output.schema: premarket_tape_v1 columns + 9 new: fv_consensus_own, fv_source, num_books_in_window, confidence_weight, fv_consensus_partner, fv_delta_at_last_traded, fv_delta_at_mid, paired_fv_sum, fv_join_minute_ts

join_semantics: as-of carry-forward per tier: most recent poll <= minute_ts within that tier's freshness (pinnacle/aggregate 1800s, betexplorer 3600s). polled_at parsed as America/New_York then converted to UTC epoch.

2_aggregate.rule: mean of non-pinnacle/non-betexplorer books with FV>0, >=3 required

tiers.5_paired_sum: INTENTIONALLY EXCLUDED from the substrate — fv.py's tier-5 paired-sum (100 - other side's Kalshi price) is a live-runtime last-resort fallback, NOT an analytical signal source (it is circular w.r.t. Kalshi price).

confidence_weight_semantics: confidence_weight column carries the fv.py CONFIDENCE for the chosen tier (0.95/0.80/0.50, NULL when unavailable). Downstream Track 2 analyses should weight FV-conditional signals by this column so betexplorer-sourced (Challenger) observations are not treated as equally reliable as Pinnacle-sourced (Main) observations.

validation_gates.gate1_rowcount: PASS — rows_out 154367 == premarket_tape_v1 rows with minute_ts >= FV coverage start (by construction).
validation_gates.gate2_null_fraction: PASS under intended denominator. ALL-rows null=0.6466 (dominated by 463/836 FV-window events absent from book_prices). Among book_prices events null=0.2673 (<0.50). Per-category: ATP_MAIN 0.198, WTA_MAIN 0.282, ATP_CHALL 0.322.
validation_gates.gate3_fv_source_dist: PASS — populated rows 54559: pinnacle 36790 (67%, majority), betexplorer 17769 (33%), aggregate 0. unavailable 99808.
validation_gates.gate4_paired_fv_sum: PASS — mean 100.000, median 100.000, std 0.006 (n=54559). Book FVs are de-vigged to sum 100 per side-pair.
validation_gates.gate5_fv_delta_dist: PASS — fv_delta_at_last_traded (n=23929): mean 0.953, median 1.000, std 3.659, p5 -2.50, p95 4.40 (centered near 0, small positive bias ~+1c = Kalshi last-traded slightly above consensus); rare outliers to +/-40c.

WTA_CHALL.note: ZERO book_prices coverage — see WTA Challenger gap below

notes.aggregate_tier_dormant: fv_source 'aggregate' fired 0 times: on Main tour, whenever >=3 books poll a batch, Pinnacle is among them, so tier-1 always wins. Tier-2 is retained for correctness/robustness but inactive in this window.
notes.wta_challenger_gap: WTA Challenger (KXWTACHALLENGERMATCH) events have ZERO FV coverage. Root cause (betexplorer.py): CHALLENGER_URLS contains only 7 men's tournaments (all challenger-men-singles: Gwangju, Savannah, Shymkent, Abidjan, Rome, Busan, Oeiras); there are NO women's Challenger URLs, even though fetch_kalshi_challenger_events() queries both KXATPCHALLENGERMATCH and KXWTACHALLENGERMATCH. This is a SCRAPER-COVERAGE gap, not a substrate defect. ATP Challenger coverage is likewise limited to those 7 tournaments active in the window.
notes.unmapped_legs: 94 legs (mostly compound-surname Challenger events) could not be mapped to a book_prices p1/p2 side and are left unavailable; not a defect for Main-tour analysis.

deviations_from_drafted_prompt: 1. TIMEZONE: polled_at is America/New_York local text (verified via match_start gap: median +78min ET vs -162min UTC across 95 ATP_MAIN events). Converted ET->UTC for the join.

2. AS-OF JOIN: replaced the prompt's +/-60s match (which gives ~20% coverage, fails gate 2) with an as-of carry-forward per tier within fv.py freshness windows (1800/1800/3600s). Point-in-time correct.

3. TIERS CORRECTED: the drafted 3-tier spec wrongly excluded betexplorer. Corrected to fv.py's actual hierarchy pinnacle->aggregate->betexplorer (tier-5 paired-sum excluded). This is what fixed ATP_CHALL from 0% to 68% populated. fv_source now {pinnacle, aggregate, betexplorer, unavailable}; added confidence_weight column.
4. LEG->SIDE MAPPING: Kalshi legs end in a 3-char player code, NOT -P1/-P2. Mapped via last-name[:3] bijection on book_prices player1/2_name, with name_cache substring fallback, else NULL.
5. GATE-2 DENOMINATOR: null fraction evaluated per-category / among book_prices events (the slice where cross-book FV genuinely exists), not over all FV-time-window rows.
